name: SPOT bounded raw-MOC fixed-point map-defect capture
purpose: Piggyback a raw primary MOC fixed-point evaluation on each of two declared one-step probe updates; the instrumentation itself adds zero operator applications.

frozen_input.reason: The two captures are parallel diagnostics and do not select or rank an acceleration scheme.

after: MCGFCF or the selected raw MOC sweep
STIS correction or ordinary volume normalization

audit_sink.publication_rule: MCGMRE may publish COMPLETE only after the first primary call returns and every one of the 370 group tuples is present in the same epoch.

root_records.name: NGIND
root_records.meaning: The active group identifiers in call order.

root_records.name: GROUP
root_records.meaning: One audit directory per energy group.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-QFR
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The same-call binary32 QFR input promoted exactly to binary64.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-EVAL
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The evaluated binary32 iteration state promoted exactly to binary64.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-SRC
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The post-MCGFCS source-element vector S. Its production binary32 inputs and arithmetic are preserved by assignment to binary64; it includes within-group scattering and boundary-current source terms.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-RAW
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The binary64 raw MOC response before ACA or SCR.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-STEP
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The locked GMRES global iteration index; it must equal one.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-ROLE
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The locked role code 1 for the primary fixed-point evaluation; it equals STATE-VECTOR role.

records_per_audit_group.name: SPOT-M-GROUP
records_per_audit_group.meaning: The physical group identifier for this tuple.

required_implementation_properties: The diagnostic switch fails closed outside TYPE S plus MCCG.
The diagnostic writes only below SPOT-MOC-AUD in the fresh writable L_FLUX output; SYSTEM remains a read-only RHS object.
The audit directory is absent before the update and is created with status INCOMPLETE.
All pre-existing SYSTEM records remain bitwise unchanged.
Diagnostic OFF reproduces the baseline solver state, standard scientific records, and normalized scientific log bit for bit; executable bytes are not compared.
Diagnostic ON reproduces every non-audit scientific record from OFF and the frozen probe_post object bit for bit.
MCGMRE passes an explicit call role and iteration; only the first primary fixed-point evaluation may write.
The seven per-group records form one same-call tuple and may not be overwritten by the affine-RHS, Krylov-basis, or later-primary calls.
The diagnostic ON and OFF paths have identical MCGFL1 and raw-sweep call counts; the capture block contains no transport call.
The audit status changes from INCOMPLETE to COMPLETE only after the locked 370-group active set is fully written.
No record is read back into the solver.
No relaxation, fitted coefficient, clipping, flux floor, or model term is introduced.

arithmetic_contract.weights: TRACK VOLUME(1..8), each binary32 value promoted exactly to binary64
arithmetic_contract.scalar_reduction_order: group 1..370 outer, region 1..8 inner, left-to-right accumulation
arithmetic_contract.relative_two_norm: sqrt(sum(((V * delta) * delta)) / sum(((V * eval) * eval)))
arithmetic_contract.input_normalized_global_max: max(abs(delta)) / max(abs(eval)); retain every exact binary64 numerator tie
arithmetic_contract.floating_point: IEEE binary64 for subtraction, products, sums, maxima, division and square root; no contraction or reassociation

required_checks: frozen parent receipt and fresh-copy identity
audit-directory pre-run absence and post-run COMPLETE publication
370-group record census, ordered NGIND and exact type/length
write-once epoch=1, role=primary, FLU IT=1, JT=1, serial=1 and MCGMRE step=1 identity
all 14 unknowns finite and the eight retained scalar fluxes positive
bitwise preservation of all non-diagnostic SYSTEM records
bitwise preservation of every non-audit L_FLUX record against OFF and frozen probe_post
binary64 recomputation of raw_minus_eval in group-major unknown-minor order
independent binary32 replay of MCGFCS from QFR, EVAL and frozen TRACK/SYSTEM inputs
source, active-set, role, epoch, phase, branch-control and capture-step identity
identical ON/OFF MCGFL1 and raw-sweep call counts
absence of Dragon, FLU, MCCG, SPOT solver symbols
reported_quantities: full 14-unknown raw_minus_eval ledger with scalar-flux or surface-current category
eight-scalar-flux-only input-normalized global maximum and all exact ties
six surface-current differences reported componentwise without a mixed flux-current norm

interpretation_limits: The captured difference is the raw-sweep map defect of the first primary evaluation at the frozen terminal input.
It is not an independently assembled matrix residual or a transport-error bound.
It does not by itself identify a unique floating-point floor.
It cannot rank the NATIVE and STATIONARY acceleration arms.
Comparing this raw-primary defect with the terminal production-map defect does not decompose or attribute ACA, GMRES, FLUBAL, FLU2AC, or rounding effects.
A REAL64 working-iteration lane is scoped only after this additional raw-sweep observable is captured.
